summary:
Claim: The recommended radiation dose for early and locally advanced cervical cancer at point A during brachytherapy has evolved to favor higher doses, specifically within the 85–90 Gy range.  
Evidence: - Major oncology guidelines (NCCN, ESMO) now recommend considering 85–90 Gy as a standard for curative-intent therapy due to proven improvements in local control. - Phase III trials confirm significantly lower recurrence rates when treating with total doses of 85–90 Gy, especially in intermediate-risk patients.  
Answer: C
